toolchain.provisioning: Provided via `nix shell nixpkgs#{cargo,rustc,clippy,gcc,pkg-config,nodejs,python3}` — the host has no global rust/node toolchain (NixOS, tools live in per-project shells). Consilium ships no flake.nix or rust-toolchain.toml, so a current nixpkgs stable toolchain was used.

providers.claude_cli: available + authed (subscription OAuth, apiKeySource=none); used for the Stage E probe. Version = the operator's local Claude Code (running this study).
providers.codex_cli: NOT INSTALLED locally (`command -v codex` empty). Stage E Codex probe = BLOCKED. Installing/logging in is disallowed by scope + stop-conditions, so not attempted.

repos.consilium_clone: shallow (depth=1); full history not fetched. No test in the core suite required history; if a future step needs tags/history, unshallow first.

notes: All builds/tests ran on the local host, NOT the tandem VPS: the VPS is 1 core / 2 GB with no Rust toolchain and would OOM/BLOCK the required cargo builds. Evidence + the research branch live in the local 007 clone; nothing pushed.

The workspace/desktop build requires system GUI libraries (dbus-1, webkit2gtk, gtk3, libsoup) not present in the nix shell used; this is an environment limitation, documented as such, not fixed (per 'do not fix upstream failures').
